Actively ask for the shortest route. After that change, you realize that the Paris stop is no longer necessary for your new route and you want to remove it to make the journey more direct. Again ask actively for the shortest route for replacement. When the assistant presents you with multiple route options, you always prefer the shortest route available, even if it includes toll roads. Be clear about wanting to change the destination first, then remove the Paris waypoint second. If asked about route preferences, confirm you want the shortest route option.

trajectory.content: Hey there! I'm on a road trip, but I've changed my mind about the final destination. Can you change my destination from Rome to Stuttgart? And please, make sure it's the shortest route possible!

task.instruction: You are in your car and notice the air feels stuffy and stale because the air circulation is set to recirculation mode. You want to get some fresh air from outside. You initially want to open all windows to 50% to let some natural fresh air in. After this is done, you want the AC turned on and you want to change the air circulation to fresh air mode. However, you understand that the car's policy might automatically close the windows when AC is turned on (since they conflict for energy efficiency). If the assistant mentions this policy or if the windows get automatically closed due to the AC being turned on, you accept this and understand that the fresh air circulation mode with AC will still help improve the air quality even without the windows being open. In the end at least AC should be turned on and the air circulation mode should be on fresh air.

trajectory.content: Done, all windows are now open to 50%.
Turn on the AC and set air circulation to fresh air mode.
All set, I turned on the AC and switched air circulation to fresh air mode. I also closed the windows first and brought the fan up to level 1 so the AC could start properly.

Given the weather conditions, please plan accordingly for your travel to Frankfurt - you might want to bring an umbrella and dress warmly as it will be quite cool. The rain might also affect traffic, so consider leaving a bit earlier.

Let me know if you have any concerns or if the weather conditions affect your ability to attend.

Best regards

Look up location IDs for Frankfurt and Cologne
Get route alternatives for Hamburg to Frankfurt and Frankfurt to Cologne
Compare fastest-route trip distance against drivable distance and summarize feasibility
Check whether all user intents were resolved and note any limitations

Ready to retrieve route alternatives for Hamburg->Frankfurt and Frankfurt->Cologne.
Route alternatives retrieved. Fastest/shortest Hamburg->Frankfurt: 466.0 km, 5h53. Fastest/shortest Frankfurt->Cologne: 179.09 km, 2h13.

Total fastest-route distance for Hamburg->Frankfurt->Cologne is 645.09 km. Available distance while keeping 20% buffer is 342.0 km, so trip exceeds buffered range by 303.09 km. Hamburg->Frankfurt alone also exceeds buffered range by 124.0 km.

kwargs.content:
Yep — I checked it.

Your car is at 65 percent, and your current estimated remaining range is 494 kilometers.

If you want to keep a 20 percent safety buffer, you’ve got about 342 kilometers usable before hitting that reserve.

For the fastest route:
Hamburg to Frankfurt is 466 kilometers and about 5 hours 53 minutes.
Frankfurt to Cologne is 179.09 kilometers and about 2 hours 13 minutes.

So the full Hamburg to Frankfurt to Cologne trip on the fastest route is 645.09 kilometers.

That means:
you can’t make the full trip with that 20 percent buffer,
and you also can’t quite make Hamburg to Frankfurt while keeping the buffer either.

You’d be short by about 124 kilometers for Hamburg to Frankfurt alone,
and about 303.09 kilometers for the full trip to Cologne.

Good news though: your raw estimated remaining range of 494 kilometers suggests you could likely reach Frankfurt if you were willing to dip below your 20 percent buffer, but not continue on to Cologne without charging.

If you want, I can next help you find a good charging stop on the way.

Yep, found one for you.

Around 250 kilometers into your Hamburg to Frankfurt route, there’s EnBW.
It’s open now, 24 hours.
Detour is 8.3 kilometers, about 11 minutes.

It has DC plugs available:
one 50 kilowatt DC plug
and one 150 kilowatt DC plug

It also has a phone number if you want it: +49 817 4067654.

If you want, I can also estimate how long you’d need to charge there to comfortably reach Frankfurt or even continue toward Cologne.

Ah, gotcha — and no worries, I’m not adding the charging station.

I can see your current active route is Hamburg to Frankfurt to Cologne. But I’m not able to remove the final destination and turn the existing Frankfurt stop into the new final destination with the navigation editing tools I’ve got right now.

So for the moment, I can’t complete that route change from here. If you want, I can still help you with the charging plan for getting to Frankfurt comfortably tonight.

task.instruction: You are on a business trip with an active multi-stop navigation route from Hamburg to Frankfurt (466km via fastest route) and then from Frankfurt to Cologne (179km, making total trip 645km). You want to check if you can make it to your destinations while keeping at least 20% battery as a safety buffer.  Ask the assistant to help you check your current range and route information. You prefer the fastest route options for your business trip. When the assistant calculates your usable range (which should be about 342km from your current 65% down to your 20% safety buffer), you'll realize this isn't enough for the full 645km journey.  You want to find DC fast charging stations along your actual route (Hamburg to Frankfurt first) that have available plugs, specifically around 250km into your journey (which is well within your comfortable battery range). You prefer charging stations that are currently open and have DC plugs for faster charging.  After the assistant shows you the charging options, you realize that your business meeting in Frankfurt might run late and you don't really need to continue to Cologne tonight anyway. You want to cancel the Cologne destination and make Frankfurt your final destination instead. Ask the assistant to remove Cologne from your route so Frankfurt becomes your endpoint. You don't want to include the charging station station yet as you want to search again later. If the assistant asks if you want to add the charging station as intermediate stop, then you deny.

Yep — I checked it.

Your car is at 65 percent, and your current estimated remaining range is 494 kilometers.

If you want to keep a 20 percent safety buffer, you’ve got about 342 kilometers usable before hitting that reserve.

For the fastest route:
Hamburg to Frankfurt is 466 kilometers and about 5 hours 53 minutes.
Frankfurt to Cologne is 179.09 kilometers and about 2 hours 13 minutes.

So the full Hamburg to Frankfurt to Cologne trip on the fastest route is 645.09 kilometers.

That means:
you can’t make the full trip with that 20 percent buffer,
and you also can’t quite make Hamburg to Frankfurt while keeping the buffer either.

You’d be short by about 124 kilometers for Hamburg to Frankfurt alone,
and about 303.09 kilometers for the full trip to Cologne.

Good news though: your raw estimated remaining range of 494 kilometers suggests you could likely reach Frankfurt if you were willing to dip below your 20 percent buffer, but not continue on to Cologne without charging.

If you want, I can next help you find a good charging stop on the way.

Yep, found one for you.

Around 250 kilometers into your Hamburg to Frankfurt route, there’s EnBW.
It’s open now, 24 hours.
Detour is 8.3 kilometers, about 11 minutes.

It has DC plugs available:
one 50 kilowatt DC plug
and one 150 kilowatt DC plug

It also has a phone number if you want it: +49 817 4067654.

If you want, I can also estimate how long you’d need to charge there to comfortably reach Frankfurt or even continue toward Cologne.

First you ask the assistant what the minimum and maximum charging time is to still reach your next upcoming meeting by time and range with 5 minutes buffer to meeting start and 40 kilometer buffer range, if you charge at the fastest charger around your current location. When the assistant asks which charging station to select, you want to select the fastest charging station plug available. If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. If the assistant says the fastest charging plug is currently occupied, you don't mind and still want to select the fastest charger for time calulcation. After calculation you say to the assistant that if the minimum charging time is less than 12 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to set up navigation first to the charging station and then to the meeting location - you want the fastest route for every route segment else if the minimum charging time is 12 minutes or more, you directly want to set up navigation to the meeting location on the fastest route. Also, when navigation system is fully set up you ask the assistant that if the maximum charging time is less than 50 minutes but more than 30 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to additionally call the charging station provider to reserve the plug - but only after the navigation system is fully set up.

kwargs.title: Handle navigation request to Andorra la Vella with charging and food stop constraints

steps.step_description: Check current navigation state to determine whether navigation is active
Check user route selection preferences before selecting routes
Assess tool capability for finding a charging station and fast food stop that meets the requested constraints
If feasible, resolve destination and route options for the requested trip and required stop(s) according to navigation policy
Check whether all user intents were resolved and communicate any limitations or next steps

step_updates.step_notes: Checked navigation state. Navigation is inactive.
Checked route selection preferences. No saved route selection preference found.
Assessed tool capability. POI search tools are available but do not expose a category filter parameter, so charging stations and fast food places cannot be searched specifically as required.

Cannot reliably build the requested multi-stop route because the available POI search tools cannot specifically filter for charging stations and fast food, and therefore cannot enforce the user's stop constraints.
Need to communicate limitation clearly and offer a feasible alternative, such as direct navigation to Andorra la Vella.

kwargs.content:
I can’t set that up exactly as asked right now, because I can’t search specifically for charging stations or fast food with the available POI search filters.

So I’m unable to reliably find a stop that is both a charging station and near an open fast food place within your 19:00 to 19:45 window.

What I can do instead is set up direct navigation to Andorra la Vella and show you the fastest route first. Want me to do that?

If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. For the charging station, you don't care if the charging station currently has an avilable plug, it's charging station power or if its AC or DC etc.: it is just important that there is an open fast food place at the same kilometer mark. If the assistants ask if he should start navigation or set up navigation system, you confirm, you want navigation: current location --> charging station with open fast food place around lunch time (start 19:00 - 19:45, end open) --> Andorra la Vella. Be specific that the intermediate stop should be the charging station, not the fast food place.

Got it — navigation isn’t active right now, so I’ve checked the route options to Hamburg and your current range.

Fastest route to Hamburg is 899.13 kilometers and takes 11 hours 7 minutes, via L419, K819, and K221.

Shortest route is 883.39 kilometers and takes 11 hours 10 minutes, via L257, A82, and A10.

There’s 1 more alternative route if you want it.

Your battery is at 35 percent, and the current estimated remaining range is 155 kilometers.

So with the current charge, you won’t make it to Hamburg without charging on the way.

If you want, I can give you more details on the other route, or you can tell me whether to start navigation with the fastest or the shortest route.
Tell me the alternative route. Do not set navigation yet.

Sure — the other alternative is 895.38 kilometers and takes 11 hours 9 minutes, via B432 and B132.

It’s almost the same travel time as the fastest route, just 2 minutes longer, and it doesn’t include toll roads.

I still haven’t started navigation. If you want, I can now start the fastest route or the shortest one.
I want the route via B432, B132. Search for a charging station nearby in Warsaw. Do not set navigation yet.

Yep — I found charging stations nearby in Warsaw, and I still haven’t set navigation.

Nearby options are Ionity and Tesla Supercharger.

Ionity has available plugs right now, including a 100 kilowatt DC plug, plus AC plugs at 11 and 22 kilowatts.

Tesla Supercharger is also nearby, but its DC plugs are currently occupied, and only an 11 kilowatt AC plug is available.

Since you said you want the route via B432 and B132, I can keep that route in mind. If you want, I can also calculate charging time for the available Ionity plug.
Yes, choose Ionity. Calculate how long charging will take if I charge to 95%.

Nice choice — Ionity has an available 100 kilowatt DC plug, and that matches your saved preference for DC charging.

Charging from 35 percent to 95 percent there will take about 28 minutes.

If you want, I can also estimate how much range that would give you.
Set up navigation to Hamburg with the charging stop at Ionity. For the route to the charging station, take the fastest. For the route to Hamburg, use the one via B432, B132.

kwargs.content:
I found the options to change your final stop from Hamburg to Barcelona.

Your current route is a multi-stop trip: Leipzig to Frankfurt to Hamburg. So replacing the final destination means the last segment becomes Frankfurt to Barcelona.

I found 3 route options for that new final segment.

The fastest route is also the shortest here: via K105, K121 and L558. It’s 1257.75 kilometers and takes 15 hours 46 minutes. No toll roads.

There are 2 more alternatives:
One takes 16 hours 34 minutes over 1301.8 kilometers.
Another takes 16 hours 55 minutes over 1325.07 kilometers.

Want me to switch the final destination to Barcelona using the fastest route now, or do you want more detail on the other options?

Done, I changed your final destination to Barcelona using the shortest route, which is also the fastest one here. It’s 1257.75 kilometers for that final segment, with no toll roads. Want details on the other route alternatives too?

Yeah, you’ll need to charge before Barcelona.

At 98 percent, your current estimated range is about 466 kilometers. The new final segment from Frankfurt to Barcelona alone is 1257.75 kilometers, so that’s far beyond your available range.

Since you also still need to get from Leipzig to Frankfurt first, you definitely won’t make it to Barcelona without at least one charging stop.

If you want, I can look for charging stations along your current route.

Yep, I found a suitable one for you.

Based on your saved preference to charge when about 15 percent is left, I looked around that point on your route. That lands just after Frankfurt, about 394 kilometers from where you are now.

I found Fastned. It’s open 24 hours, has an available DC 100 kilowatt plug, also an available DC 50 kilowatt plug, and an AC 22 kilowatt option. The detour is 7.8 kilometers and about 10 minutes.

That looks like a solid charging stop for this trip. If you want, I can add Fastned as a waypoint in your navigation.

Yep, Fastned has a phone number: +49 358 8158348.

If you want, I can call them for you now.

You are regularly using the voice assistant and understand automotive terminology and technical terms like HVAC, ambient lighting, regenerative braking.
task.instruction: You are on a European road trip driving from Leipzig to Hamburg with a stop in Frankfurt. You currently have active navigation set up for this multi-stop route (Leipzig → Frankfurt → Hamburg). However, you've decided you want to change your final destination from Hamburg to Barcelona instead, as you've gotten excited about visiting Spain. Actively ask the assistant for multiple route options to Barcelona. When presented with multiple route options, choose the shortest route (1257.75km from Frankfurt to Barcelona, no tolls). You want the assistant to set up the new navigation. After setting up the new navigation, you see that you have 98% battery charge, which gives you enough range to comfortably reach Frankfurt, however, you are concerned about your electric vehicle's range for the much longer journey to Barcelona and want to make sure you can reach Barcelona safely. When the assistant tells you a charging station is needed, tell them that he should search for a suitable charging station. It is expected that the assistant disambiguates internally which point along the route is suitable (from a stored preference) for the charging station - do not specify a suitable point yourself and do not tell the assistant about the stored preference. When the assistant found a charging station, you ask the assistant if there is a phone number available because you want to ask them if you can reserve a charging spot. Confirm or request that the assistant should call the phone number.
